# `ddx agent` surface & dead-code audit

Date: 2026-05-01. Branch: main @ b543f084.

## TL;DR

- `ddx agent` is **NOT gone**. 21 subcommands still register, 100% live (no `Hidden: true`, no `// Deprecated:` markers anywhere in `cli/internal/agent` or `cli/cmd/agent_*.go`).
- Only one of the planned three-layer commands ships at root: `ddx work`. There is **no `ddx run`** and **no `ddx try`** — the FEAT-006 retire/replace plan has not landed for the user-facing surface; documentation in `website/content/` already advertises `ddx run`/`ddx try` as if they exist.
- `cli/internal/agent/` carries ~12 truly-dead production functions and ~5 dead helper funcs in `cli/cmd/agent_*.go` (deadcode -test scope). Several stem from refactor leftovers (especially in `execute_bead_loop.go`, `grade.go`, `routing_metrics.go`).

---

## Phase A — `ddx agent` subcommand inventory

Source: `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:67-85` (registration block). Top-level `ddx agent` returns Help. All children are public, none hidden, none deprecated.

| Subcommand                | Registered at line | Role / status          | Notes |
| ------------------------- | ------------------ | ---------------------- | ----- |
| `agent run`               | 67                 | **Required (legacy)**  | Should be replaced by `ddx run`. Heavily referenced by tests, README, docs, AGENTS.md. No top-level alias yet. |
| `agent condense`          | 68                 | Required (niche)       | `agent_cmd.go:419`. No `ddx condense` alias. |
| `agent list`              | 69                 | Required               | Harness inventory. |
| `agent capabilities`      | 70                 | Required               | Per-harness probe. |
| `agent doctor`            | 71                 | Required               | Health check. |
| `agent log`               | 72                 | Required               | Session history; has `agent log reindex` subcommand. |
| `agent benchmark`         | 73                 | **Likely dead**        | No production callers; `DefaultBenchmarkArms` test-only; quorum/grade chain (see Phase B). |
| `agent usage`             | 74                 | Required               | Token consumption (cmd has 3 dead helpers). |
| `agent replay`            | 75                 | Required (low traffic) | Virtual harness replay. |
| `agent execute-bead`      | 76                 | **Required (legacy)**  | Should be replaced by `ddx try` per FEAT-006. Top-level `ddx try` not implemented. |
| `agent execute-loop`      | 77                 | **Deprecated alias**   | `ddx work` already wraps the same code path. Doc text at agent_cmd.go:1504-1555 still recommends it as primary in places, contradicting `cli/_index.md` "prefer ddx work". |
| `agent executions`        | 78                 | Required               | Mirror fetch CLI. |

| `agent check`             | 83                 | Required               | Static analyses. |
| `agent route-status`      | 84                 | Required               | Routing telemetry. |
| `agent metrics`           | 85                 | Required               | Analytics over evidence. |

**Verdict on Phase A:** the deprecation plan from `/tmp/execute-bead-refactor-proposal.md` §3.3 has not been executed. Three-layer plan delivered only `ddx work`; `ddx run` and `ddx try` do not exist. Nothing in the agent group is marked Hidden or Deprecated.

### Cross-references that block deletion today

- README.md (lines 77, 80, 83, 87, 99-100), CHANGELOG.md, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, website/content/docs/* — all promote `ddx agent run/usage/execute-bead/log/list/doctor` as the canonical surface.
- 32 `cli/cmd/agent_*_test.go` files exec via `NewRootCommand()` and assert `ddx agent <X>` paths.
- Skill manifests under `.agents/skills/` and `.claude/skills/` reference `ddx agent` commands — needs review when surfaces are renamed.
- `cli/cmd/bead_review.go:30-33` long-help suggests `ddx agent run --prompt @-`.

No production code path under `cli/internal/server/` execs the `ddx agent` binary as a subprocess; the server uses the `cli/internal/agent` Go package directly. Subprocess invocations of `ddx agent` are limited to docs/skill prompts.

## Phase D — Cleanup proposal (bead-sized chunks)

### Bead 1 — Retire `adjudicateNoChanges` and its dead AC-claim helpers
- Delete `ExecuteBeadWorker.adjudicateNoChanges` (`execute_bead_loop.go:1427-1500ish`).
- Delete `VerifyACClaims`, `goFieldDeclared` (`ac_structural_check.go:88-end`).
- Audit `ParseACClaims` — if its only remaining caller is `adjudicateNoChanges`, delete it too. Otherwise keep.
- Update / remove `ac_structural_check_test.go`, `ac_structural_check_operator_prompt_test.go`.
- Scope: ~250 LOC + tests.

### Bead 2 — Collapse `agent_usage.go` to the monthly-shard reader
- Delete `aggregateUsage`, `aggregateUsageAggs`, `readUsageSessionRecords` (`agent_usage.go:245-540`).
- Confirm `usageRow`, `usageAgg`, `usageSessionRecord` still have callers post-deletion; remove if not.
- Verify `agent_usage_test.go` doesn't exercise the dead helpers; if it does, delete those tests.
- Scope: ~300 LOC + tests.

### Bead 3 — Wire (not delete) the recent escalation / retry-policy work
**Different shape from the others — this is "use it or lose it."** Either:
- Wire `EvaluateRetryPolicy`, `RunWithLockBackoff`, `DispatchPostAttempt`, `escalation.Ladder.Next` into the production loop in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` (preferred — that was the point of those features), or
- Delete them and their tests.
- Affected files: `retry_policy.go`, `triage_dispatch.go`, `escalation/ladder.go` plus tests.
- Recommend wiring; flag for owner decision before any deletion. **Highest risk of false-positive removal.**

### Bead 4 — Drop quorum/benchmark formatting + grading dead paths
- Delete `format.go` (`FormatComparisonTable`, `FormatGradeTable`).
- Delete `grade.go:buildGradingPrompt` (un-bounded variant superseded by `buildGradingPromptBounded`).
- Delete `model_catalog_yaml.go:WriteModelCatalogYAML`.
- Delete `routing_metrics.go:AppendBurnSummary`, `parseWindowMinutes`.
- Delete `harnessHealthyViaService` in `agent_cmd.go`, `loadExecutionsMirrorConfig` in `agent_execute_bead.go`.
- Drop unused test helpers: `gitHasMergeCommits`, `refExists`, `countOccurrences`.
- Scope: ~500 LOC, low risk.

### Bead 5 — Begin the actual `ddx agent` deprecation per FEAT-006
- Implement `ddx run` as the layer-1 alias (current `ddx agent run`).
- Implement `ddx try` as the layer-2 single-bead command (current `ddx agent execute-bead`).
- Mark `ddx agent run`, `ddx agent execute-bead`, `ddx agent execute-loop` as `Hidden: true` + add `// Deprecated:` comments and emit a stderr deprecation warning.
- Update README.md, CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, CHANGELOG, website/content/docs/cli/_index.md and feature pages to lead with `ddx run / try / work`.
- Update skill manifests under `.agents/skills/`, `.claude/skills/` to use the new surface.
- Scope: large; spans CLI + docs + skills. May need to be 2 beads (CLI + docs/skills).

### Bead 6 (optional) — Hide / consolidate niche `ddx agent` subcommands
- `agent benchmark` (no production users; no `cli/internal/agent/grade.go writeGradeArtifacts` flow exercised in tests; verify before keeping).
- `agent condense`, `agent capabilities`, `agent replay` — confirm callers; consider hiding from top-level help.

## Risks / false positives

1. **`cli/internal/agent/escalation` and `triage_dispatch.go` and `retry_policy.go`** — flagged as unreachable without `-test`, but they are test-covered new feature work landed within the past 3 days. **Do not delete.** Either wire into prod or escalate to owner.
2. **`MinTier` field in `internal/config/resolved.go`** — name overlaps with the retired adaptive-min-tier concept but is the live MinPower CLI-override field. Keep.
3. **`virtual` harness paths** — appear lightly used; they are the record/replay surface for tests and `agent replay`. Keep (`runner.go:103`, `harness_config.go:153`, `service_run.go:64`).
4. **`RunQuorumWithConfig`** — flagged dead; production calls `RunQuorumWithConfigViaService`. The non-Service variant is genuinely unused. Safe to delete *with* `format.go`, but check `agent_quorum_config_test.go` first — if any test pins `RunQuorumWithConfig` directly, port it to the Service variant.
5. **Skill manifests under `.agents/skills/` and `.claude/skills/`** — these are consumed by Claude/agent harnesses at runtime; renaming `ddx agent run` → `ddx run` requires updating dozens of skill prompts in lockstep. Don't delete the legacy command alias until skills are migrated.
6. **Reflection / build-tagged callers** — `cli/internal/agent/live_harness_integration_test.go` and `no_live_harness_in_default_suite_test.go` use build tags. Run `deadcode` once per build-tag set if doing a hard purge (not done here; this audit is read-only).

# Bead Authoring Template

> A bead's description + AC must be sufficient context for a competent
> sub-agent to execute it without hand-curation.
> — Reliability principle **P7** (`docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md`)

This document is the canonical template + rubric for creating beads in
this repository. It exists because the 2026-05-03 bead-quality audit
(`.ddx/executions/20260503T155638-bead57f0cb9e/bead-quality-audit-2026-05-03.md`)
found that only **1 of 20** representative open beads scored 8/8 on
the sufficient-sub-agent-prompt rubric. The most common failure modes
were: no specific test names in AC (80 % miss), no `go test` +
lefthook gate in AC (80 % miss), and no root cause with file:line in
description (65 % miss).

---

## 1. The 8-criterion rubric

Every bead must satisfy:

| # | Criterion | Verifier |
|---|-----------|----------|
| (a) | **Title**: one-line scope clarity, imperative, names subsystem + change | "Pagination off-by-one in bead list endpoint" not "Pagination bug" |
| (b) | **Description**: problem + ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE + proposed fix + non-scope | Root cause cites `cli/path/file.go:42-50` |
| (c) | **AC**: numbered, verifiable, includes specific test names | At least one AC names `TestFoo` or a `go test -run` filter |
| (d) | **AC** includes "wired-in" assertion when introducing new code paths | New function → AC asserts a caller invokes it (production or test) |
| (e) | **AC** includes `go test` command + lefthook gate | Both `cd cli && go test ./...` and `lefthook run pre-commit` named |
| (f) | **Labels**: phase + area + kind + cross-refs | `phase:N`, `area:*`, `kind:*`, plus adr/spec/prevention if applicable |
| (g) | **Parent + Deps**: explicit | Parent set; deps either listed or "no deps" stated |
| (h) | Description reads as **sufficient sub-agent prompt** | A competent agent given only the bead body can pick a file and run tests without asking |

Score 8/8 = execution-ready. Less = retrofit before dispatch.

---

## 2. Template fields

```text
TITLE
  <area>: <imperative one-line scope>
  e.g. "agent: unify ExecuteLoopSpec across cobra/HTTP/server layers"

TYPE
  task | bug | epic | chore

LABELS
  phase:<N>, area:<subsystem>, kind:<category>
  + cross-refs: prevention, adr:<id>, spec:<id>, story:<n>, refactor

PARENT
  <ddx-id> of governing epic / story / spec
  (omit only if this is a root)

DEPS
  - <ddx-id-1>: <one-line why this dep matters>
  - <ddx-id-2>: <one-line why this dep matters>
  (or explicit "No deps.")

SPEC-ID (when applicable)
  --set spec-id=FEAT-NNN  | SD-NNN | TD-NNN | ADR-NNN

DESCRIPTION
  PROBLEM
    <one-paragraph statement of what is broken or missing>

  ROOT CAUSE
    <function/file:line where the defect lives>
    <what the code does today vs what it should do>
    For new features: ROOT CAUSE -> "no existing implementation"
    is OK; replace with CURRENT STATE describing the gap.

  PROPOSED FIX
    <bulleted concrete changes; cite file:line for each>
    - cli/internal/foo/bar.go:120-145  rename Baz -> Quux
    - cli/internal/foo/bar_test.go      add TestQuux_RoundTrip

  NON-SCOPE
    - <thing readers might assume is included but isn't>
    - <thing covered by sister bead ddx-NNNN>

  INTERSECTIONS (optional)
    - <ddx-id>: <how this bead relates to that one>

ACCEPTANCE
  1. <verifiable assertion 1 — names a Test, file path, command, or
     observable artifact>
  2. <verifiable assertion 2>
  3. ...
  N-1. cd cli && go test ./<paths>/... green.
  N. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
```

---

## 3. Examples

### 3.1 Example of a strong bead (audit score 8/8)

`ddx-1e516bc9` — *"fizeau Execute: explicit Harness + empty Model + non-empty Profile/MinPower must route within harness's eligible models"*

Why it scored 8/8:

- **Title (a)** names subsystem (`fizeau Execute`), the precondition (`explicit Harness + empty Model`), and the expected behavior (`route within harness's eligible models`).
- **Description (b)** opens with **OBSERVED** (one paragraph, file:line) → **SCOPE — UPSTREAM (fizeau) ONLY** → **INVESTIGATION FIRST** (with explicit close-as-no-op outcome) → **UPSTREAM CHANGE** (file:line into `service_execute.go:193-224`, `models.yaml:31-60`, `registry.go`) → explicit **NOT IN SCOPE** list naming five excluded paths.
- **AC (c)** names two concrete tests:
  `TestExecute_ExplicitHarnessEmptyModelWithProfile_RoutesWithinHarness` and
  `TestExecute_ExplicitHarnessEmptyModelNoProfile_FailsClearly`.
- **Wired-in (d)**: AC #6 asserts the resolved model surfaces in
  `.ddx/workers/<id>/spec.json`, `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/result.json`, and the
  workers UI — three distinct call-graph endpoints.
- **Test command + lefthook (e)**: AC #9 — `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... green; lefthook pre-commit passes.`
- **Labels (f)**: `phase:2, area:agent, kind:fix, upstream-fizeau, observed-failure`.
- **Parent + Deps (g)**: parent `ddx-e34994e2`; dep `ddx-cfedee8e` with explanation that the ladder must supply MinPower for fizeau to act on.
- **Sufficient (h)**: a sub-agent given only the bead body knows which file in fizeau to read, what the gap looks like, what the negative-test outcome should be, and how to verify the end-to-end fix without asking.

### 3.2 Example of a weak bead (audit score 1/8) — annotated

`ddx-0793ac75` — *"federation: federated GraphQL resolvers + routing-metadata fields"*

Verbatim description:
```
GraphQL resolvers that aggregate across spokes (federatedWorkers,
federatedRuns, federatedDocuments, etc.). Add routing-metadata fields
exposing which spoke owns each result. Write-routing contract: hub
forwards mutations to owning node, never broadcasts (Story 15
dependency).
```

Verbatim AC:
```
1. federated* resolvers in schema.
2. Routing-metadata visible per result.
3. Mutations route to owner; tested.
4. Tests cover happy + degraded paths.
```

Failures, criterion by criterion:

- **(b) FAIL** — no problem statement (what's broken today?), no root cause, no file:line, no non-scope. The reader cannot tell where the schema lives, where resolvers go, or what "routing-metadata" should look like as a Go type.
- **(c) FAIL** — "tested" and "Tests cover happy + degraded paths" name no `Test*` function. Sub-agent has no way to know what to write or where.
- **(d) PARTIAL** — "tested" implies the resolvers are wired, but no production-call-graph assertion (e.g., "schema.graphql Mutation block exposes field X").
- **(e) FAIL** — no `go test` command, no lefthook gate.
- **(h) FAIL** — a sub-agent reading this cold cannot answer: which schema file? which resolver package? what fields on routing-metadata? what's the wire format? Hand-curation by the operator is the only path forward.

**Retrofit shape** (illustrative — not an actual edit):
```
PROBLEM
  Story 14 needs aggregate-across-spokes resolvers. Today the GraphQL
  schema (cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql) exposes only
  per-node Worker/Run/Document queries. Federation requires
  federated* counterparts that fan out to spokes and stitch results.

ROOT CAUSE
  No federated resolver layer exists. cli/internal/server/graphql_resolver.go
  contains only single-node resolvers. Spoke fan-out is in
  cli/internal/server/federation_spoke.go but nothing in the GraphQL
  layer calls it.

PROPOSED FIX
  - schema.graphql: add Federated{Worker,Run,Document} types with
    `ownerSpoke: String!` field.
  - graphql_resolver.go: add federatedWorkers/federatedRuns/
    federatedDocuments resolvers calling federation_spoke.FanOut.
  - graphql_mutation.go: write-routing helper routes mutations to
    owning node via federation_spoke.RouteToOwner.

NON-SCOPE
  - New non-federated resolvers (out: covered by Story 13).
  - Spoke discovery (out: covered by ddx-fe63969f).

ACCEPTANCE
  1. schema.graphql exposes federatedWorkers/federatedRuns/
     federatedDocuments queries with ownerSpoke metadata.
  2. TestFederatedWorkers_FanOutAggregates verifies fan-out + stitch.
  3. TestFederatedMutation_RoutesToOwner verifies write-routing.
  4. TestFederatedWorkers_DegradedSpoke returns partial results +
     warning.
  5. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... green.
  6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
```

---

## 4. Authoring checklist (paste into your editor before running `ddx bead create`)

```text
[ ] (a) Title: imperative, one line, names subsystem + change
[ ] (b) Description has all four sub-elements:
        [ ] PROBLEM (one paragraph)
        [ ] ROOT CAUSE with file:line citation (or CURRENT STATE for new features)
        [ ] PROPOSED FIX (bulleted, file:line per item)
        [ ] NON-SCOPE
[ ] (c) Each AC line is verifiable; at least one names Test* or `go test -run`
[ ] (d) New code path → AC asserts a production or test caller invokes it
[ ] (e) AC includes:
        [ ] cd cli && go test ./<paths>/... green
        [ ] lefthook run pre-commit passes
[ ] (f) Labels: phase:<N>, area:<subsystem>, kind:<category>, +cross-refs
[ ] (g) Parent set; deps listed or "no deps" stated
[ ] (h) Self-test: re-read the bead from cold. Could a competent
        sub-agent execute it without asking a question? If no, retrofit.
[ ] No /tmp/ paths cited as load-bearing context. Inline the relevant
    excerpt instead — /tmp does not survive between machines.
```

---

## 5. Anti-patterns surfaced by the audit

- **`/tmp/` references as load-bearing context.** 5 of 20 audited beads cite a `/tmp/...` plan file. These are functionally broken links to a sub-agent on a different machine. **Inline the excerpt.**
- **"See parent" without inline summary.** Refactor children of `ddx-5cb6e6cd` (C4/C7/C11/C13) systematically defer detail to the parent epic. Each child fails the stand-alone test. **Inline the parent's relevant 5-10 lines.**
- **Vague test phrases.** "Tests cover X." "All execute-bead tests still pass." Both omit the actual `Test*` name or `-run` filter the executor needs.
- **Story-doc copy-paste.** One-paragraph beads (`ddx-0793ac75`, `ddx-0e5c3005`, `ddx-1d52a30c`, `ddx-0ca4ffda`) are story excerpts that were never expanded for execution. Story documents are *upstream* of beads, not the bead itself.
- **Implicit lefthook.** Lefthook runs in pre-commit hooks anyway, but if AC doesn't name it, executors omit it from their own verification before committing.

---

## 6. When to skip a criterion

- **Epic beads** are aggregates by design. Criterion (c) (specific test names) and (d) (wired-in) commonly pass via children. The epic should still satisfy (a), (b), (e) — pointing at the children's collective `go test` set — and (f), (g), (h).
- **Doc-only beads** legitimately omit (c) (no tests) and (d) (no code path). They must still satisfy (e)'s lefthook clause and (h).
- **Pure deletion / rename beads** legitimately omit (d). They must still cite the deletion-target's file:line in (b) and assert byte-identical behavior preservation in (c).

cli/internal/agent/testdata/benchmark-implementation.json:18:      "prompt": "Read cli/internal/agent/types.go to understand the ComparisonRecord, ComparisonArm, and ComparisonGrade types. Then create a new file cli/internal/agent/format.go (package agent) containing:

1. func FormatComparisonTable(record *ComparisonRecord) string — formats the comparison as an ASCII table with columns: Arm, Status (OK/FAIL), Model, Tokens (in/out), Cost, Duration. Include a totals row.

2. func FormatGradeTable(record *ComparisonRecord) string — formats grades as a table with columns: Arm, Score, Pass, Rationale (truncated to 60 chars).

Both functions should use tabwriter for alignment. Include proper imports. Do not create test files."

data.text: The string search only finds dead references in benchmark testdata, which is expected. I’m waiting for the full `go test` and `go build` runs to finish so I can either close this cleanly or capture a real failure if one appears.

`go test ./...` failed in the `cmd` installation acceptance suite, and the failure is environmental rather than from the dead-code cleanup: the test harness cannot find `cli/build/ddx`, so it substitutes a mock binary and then fails the install-path assertions. `go build ./...` already completed cleanly.

The failing tests are looking for a prebuilt `cli/build/ddx` binary, so I’m checking the repo’s normal build path. If there’s a standard way this tree populates that artifact, I can use it and rerun the suite instead of treating the failure as fixed.

cli/internal/agent/testdata/reviewer/bead-jsonl-oversized-line.jsonl:1:{"acceptance":"- [ ] JWT middleware installed in data-plane router as optional layer (runs when Authorization header present)
- [ ] JwtIssuer constructed at startup in serve.rs (dev: random key with warning)
- [ ] AuditAttribution populated on every audit write when ResolvedIdentity is present
- [ ] gRPC service.rs migrated from x-axon-database to composite x-axon-tenant-database
- [ ] gRPC service.rs gained optional JWT verify path
- [ ] cutover_jwt_test.rs integration test with real JWT end-to-end
- [ ] scripts/cutover-smoke.sh script runs end-to-end against dev server
- [ ] cargo test --workspace green
- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean
- [ ] Legacy auth paths (--no-auth, --guest-role, Tailscale) still work unchanged","closing_commit_sha":"1a378fe785612ab9a24241977cf60c0c13e150b9","created_at":"2026-04-15T11:54:30.489788638Z","description":"Finish wiring the ADR-018 auth stack into the real request pipeline. After the smoke test on commit e83cf02, D3 already landed more than expected: `resolve_tenant_handler` composes `{tenant}:{database}` into the TenantRouter slug, giving real per-pair physical isolation. What remains is the three things that are *built but not installed*.

## What is already live (confirmed by smoke test)

- Path-based routing: /tenants/:t/databases/:d/... routed to per-pair SQLite files via composite slug
- GraphQL under the nested path
- Unprefixed REST + /graphql return 404
- HttpAxonClient in the SDK + the UI use the new paths
- Legacy X-Axon-Database header is gone from REST

## What is still NOT installed (the gap this bead closes)

### 1. JWT pipeline not enforced

B1 landed `auth_pipeline::jwt_verify_layer` + `JwtIssuer`. The real gateway router in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` does NOT install this layer. The server currently runs in legacy auth mode (Tailscale or --no-auth). Install the layer as an OPTIONAL middleware: if the request has an Authorization header, run the JWT pipeline; if not, fall back to legacy auth.

Wire point: in `build_router_with_auth` (or wherever the data-plane nested router is constructed), add `.layer(from_fn_with_state(state.clone(), jwt_verify_layer))` before the tenant_handler middleware. The layer needs access to a `JwtIssuer` and the `StorageAdapter`. Add them to the AppState struct.

Create the JwtIssuer at server startup in `serve.rs`. Read the signing key from an env var `AXON_JWT_KEY` (base64 HMAC secret); if unset, generate a random 32-byte key at startup and log a warning (dev mode). Production key management is a future concern.

The legacy auth path (crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs Identity + Role) still works for requests without an Authorization header — the JWT layer is a no-op pass-through when the header is absent. This preserves --no-auth, --guest-role, and Tailscale whois behavior exactly as today.

### 2. Audit attribution not populated

F1 added `AuditAttribution` as an optional field on `AuditEntry` plus the `with_attribution()` builder. No handler currently calls it. In the JWT middleware (step 1), after `ResolvedIdentity` is installed into the request extension, also expose a `current_attribution()` helper on AppState that the audit write sites can call.

Find every `AuditEntry::new(...)` call site in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` (there are probably 10-20) and chain `.with_attribution(current_attribution(&request))` when a ResolvedIdentity is present. When absent (legacy auth), leave the attribution as None — the existing `actor: String` field still works.

### 3. gRPC service.rs still reads x-axon-database

`crates/axon-server/src/service.rs:26` has `const AXON_DATABASE_HEADER: &str = "x-axon-database"` and `grpc_current_database` reads it from metadata. The gRPC service is entirely separate from the REST gateway and D3 did not touch it. Replace this header read with path-based extraction... except gRPC doesn't have URL paths in the same way. gRPC calls use service + method names, not paths. The database has to come from either:

(a) A metadata field (still a header-equivalent), OR
(b) A new field on every request proto

(a) is pragmatic. Rename the metadata header from `x-axon-database` to `x-axon-tenant-database` and carry a composite value `"{tenant}:{database}"`. This matches the REST composite slug. The gRPC service parses it and dispatches via the same `get_or_create_any` path.

Also add `x-axon-authorization` (or reuse the standard `authorization` metadata key) for JWT credentials. Run the JWT verify pipeline in the gRPC `check_auth` step before dispatching to the handler.

For this bead, the gRPC change is small:
- Rename the metadata constant
- Update `grpc_current_database` to parse the composite form
- Update the Rust + TS gRPC clients to send the composite form
- Add JWT auth path in `check_auth`

### 4. DatabaseRouter NOT swapped in (deferred — not needed right now)

D2 landed `DatabaseRouter` as a new primitive, but the gateway still uses `TenantRouter::get_or_create_any(slug)`. The smoke test confirmed this composite slug works correctly. Replacing TenantRouter with DatabaseRouter is a refactor that doesn't change behavior — defer it to a later cleanup bead. Not in this bead's scope.

## Scope summary

This bead does exactly three things:

1. Install B1's JWT middleware in the data-plane router, optional path (Authorization header present)
2. Populate F1's AuditAttribution at every audit write site when ResolvedIdentity is present
3. Migrate gRPC service.rs from x-axon-database header to the composite slug + optional JWT metadata

## Tests for this bead

### Integration test: crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs (NEW)

- create a JwtIssuer with a known key
- mint a JWT for a default user with grants on (default, default)
- start the in-process test server harness that gateway_test.rs uses
- send a request with Authorization: Bearer <jwt> to /tenants/default/databases/default/collections
- assert 200 (the JWT pipeline accepted + the handler saw ResolvedIdentity)

### Integration test: attribution populated

- send an entity-create request with a JWT
- query the audit log
- assert the audit entry has `attribution.user_id` set to the JWT sub

### gRPC test: composite slug

- send a CreateEntity gRPC request with metadata `x-axon-tenant-database: acme:orders`
- assert routing to the acme:orders database (check the storage file exists)

### Manual smoke test

Start the server, issue a JWT via `/control/tenants/default/credentials`, use the HttpAxonClient with the JWT, create an entity, verify the audit log has attribution. Write a short shell script under `scripts/cutover-smoke.sh` that does this end-to-end.

## Out of scope

- DatabaseRouter replacement — deferred
- OIDC federation — follow-up
- Signing key rotation — future ADR
- Removing legacy Identity/Role types from auth.rs — they still serve the --no-auth and Tailscale paths

## Files to touch

- crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs — install JWT middleware, thread AuditAttribution into audit writes (10-20 sites)
- crates/axon-server/src/serve.rs — construct JwtIssuer at startup
- crates/axon-server/src/service.rs — rename header constant, parse composite, add JWT path
- crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs — NEW
- scripts/cutover-smoke.sh — NEW
- Possibly sdk/typescript/src/client.ts — update gRPC metadata key if touching gRPC SDK surface

## Files NOT to touch

- crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs — B1 is canonical, just consume it
- crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs — legacy Tailscale path, out of scope for removal
- crates/axon-storage/* — no storage changes
- crates/axon-core/* — no type changes

## Verification

File: docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-002-web-stack.md:147
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The updated ADR now shows the embedded Playwright config launching `${cliRoot}/build/ddx server --port 18080`, but the run instructions still say only `bun run test:e2e`. That command does not build `cli/build/ddx` or refresh the embedded frontend bundle. On a clean checkout, or after frontend changes, following the ADR literally can fail because the binary is missing or stale. CI avoids this by running `bun run build` in `cli/internal/server/frontend` and `make build` in `cli` before `bun run test:e2e`.
Suggested fix: Update ADR-002's Playwright run instructions to state the required build prerequisites explicitly, or change the documented workflow to invoke a command that builds the frontend bundle and CLI binary before running Playwright.","acceptance":"ADR-002 documents the required preconditions for `bun run test:e2e` so the steps mention building the frontend bundle and `cli/build/ddx` first, consistent with `cli/internal/server/frontend/playwright.config.ts` and CI.","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding"],"bead":{"id":"hx-8f988a16","title":"correctness: document Playwright build prerequisites","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.

Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: `checkInstalledPlugins` now skips any installed entry whose `type` is empty unless the package name exists in `BuiltinRegistry()`. That fixes resource false-positives, but it also suppresses `ddx doctor --plugins` for legacy or third-party plugin installs whose older `installed.yaml` rows lack `type`. The project design explicitly keeps a builtin-registry fallback for legacy packages and expects old packages to remain auditable during the migration period, so this filter drops coverage for a supported compatibility path.

./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:237:{"id":"corpus-fd7b2f73d368","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"execute-bead: audit and enforce per-request HTTP deadlines on agent upstream-service calls","description":"## Root cause RC4 from ddx-0a651925

After RC1 is fixed (caller ctx propagates), upstream-service HTTP calls still need per-request deadlines. A stalled TCP socket that delivers headers and then stops sending body bytes — particularly during streaming token reads — can hang a goroutine for days.

Grepping the `DocumentDrivenDX/agent@v0.3.14` module cache shows short timeouts only on discovery probes (`upstream-service/openai/discovery.go:46,174`, `openai.go:123`). The main `Run` call path inside agent upstream-services appears to inherit whatever ctx DDx passes — which today is `context.Background()` (see RC1).

## Scope

1. Audit `openai`, `anthropic`, `virtual` upstream-services in the agent library for per-request deadline usage.
2. Install a per-request deadline (default ~10-15 min) on each upstream-service call; separate from the wall-clock bound in RC2.
3. For streaming bodies, enforce an **idle-read limit** on the response body so a mid-stream stall cannot hold a goroutine indefinitely (e.g. `http.Transport.ResponseHeaderTimeout` + periodic `SetReadDeadline` on the underlying conn).
4. Surface upstream-service-timeout failures as a distinct status in `ExecuteBeadResult.Detail`.

## Non-goals

- Rewriting upstream-services; minimally invasive change only.
- Changing DDx-side timeout defaults (RC2 covers outer bound).

## Prerequisite

./cli/internal/agent/testdata/reviewer/bead-jsonl-oversized-line.jsonl:1:{"acceptance":"- [ ] JWT middleware installed in data-plane router as optional layer (runs when Authorization header present)
- [ ] JwtIssuer constructed at startup in serve.rs (dev: random key with warning)
- [ ] AuditAttribution populated on every audit write when ResolvedIdentity is present
- [ ] gRPC service.rs migrated from x-axon-database to composite x-axon-tenant-database
- [ ] gRPC service.rs gained optional JWT verify path
- [ ] cutover_jwt_test.rs integration test with real JWT end-to-end
- [ ] scripts/cutover-smoke.sh script runs end-to-end against dev server
- [ ] cargo test --workspace green
- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean
- [ ] Legacy auth paths (--no-auth, --guest-role, Tailscale) still work unchanged","closing_commit_sha":"1a378fe785612ab9a24241977cf60c0c13e150b9","created_at":"2026-04-15T11:54:30.489788638Z","description":"Finish wiring the ADR-018 auth stack into the real request pipeline. After the smoke test on commit e83cf02, D3 already landed more than expected: `resolve_tenant_handler` composes `{tenant}:{database}` into the TenantRouter slug, giving real per-pair physical isolation. What remains is the three things that are *built but not installed*.

## What is already live (confirmed by smoke test)

- Path-based routing: /tenants/:t/databases/:d/... routed to per-pair SQLite files via composite slug
- GraphQL under the nested path
- Unprefixed REST + /graphql return 404
- HttpAxonClient in the SDK + the UI use the new paths
- Legacy X-Axon-Database header is gone from REST

## What is still NOT installed (the gap this bead closes)

### 1. JWT pipeline not enforced

B1 landed `auth_pipeline::jwt_verify_layer` + `JwtIssuer`. The real gateway router in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` does NOT install this layer. The server currently runs in legacy auth mode (Tailscale or --no-auth). Install the layer as an OPTIONAL middleware: if the request has an Authorization header, run the JWT pipeline; if not, fall back to legacy auth.

Wire point: in `build_router_with_auth` (or wherever the data-plane nested router is constructed), add `.layer(from_fn_with_state(state.clone(), jwt_verify_layer))` before the tenant_handler middleware. The layer needs access to a `JwtIssuer` and the `StorageAdapter`. Add them to the AppState struct.

Create the JwtIssuer at server startup in `serve.rs`. Read the signing key from an env var `AXON_JWT_KEY` (base64 HMAC secret); if unset, generate a random 32-byte key at startup and log a warning (dev mode). Production key management is a future concern.

The legacy auth path (crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs Identity + Role) still works for requests without an Authorization header — the JWT layer is a no-op pass-through when the header is absent. This preserves --no-auth, --guest-role, and Tailscale whois behavior exactly as today.

### 2. Audit attribution not populated

F1 added `AuditAttribution` as an optional field on `AuditEntry` plus the `with_attribution()` builder. No handler currently calls it. In the JWT middleware (step 1), after `ResolvedIdentity` is installed into the request extension, also expose a `current_attribution()` helper on AppState that the audit write sites can call.

Find every `AuditEntry::new(...)` call site in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` (there are probably 10-20) and chain `.with_attribution(current_attribution(&request))` when a ResolvedIdentity is present. When absent (legacy auth), leave the attribution as None — the existing `actor: String` field still works.

### 3. gRPC service.rs still reads x-axon-database

`crates/axon-server/src/service.rs:26` has `const AXON_DATABASE_HEADER: &str = "x-axon-database"` and `grpc_current_database` reads it from metadata. The gRPC service is entirely separate from the REST gateway and D3 did not touch it. Replace this header read with path-based extraction... except gRPC doesn't have URL paths in the same way. gRPC calls use service + method names, not paths. The database has to come from either:

(a) A metadata field (still a header-equivalent), OR
(b) A new field on every request proto

(a) is pragmatic. Rename the metadata header from `x-axon-database` to `x-axon-tenant-database` and carry a composite value `"{tenant}:{database}"`. This matches the REST composite slug. The gRPC service parses it and dispatches via the same `get_or_create_any` path.

Also add `x-axon-authorization` (or reuse the standard `authorization` metadata key) for JWT credentials. Run the JWT verify pipeline in the gRPC `check_auth` step before dispatching to the handler.

For this bead, the gRPC change is small:
- Rename the metadata constant
- Update `grpc_current_database` to parse the composite form
- Update the Rust + TS gRPC clients to send the composite form
- Add JWT auth path in `check_auth`

### 4. DatabaseRouter NOT swapped in (deferred — not needed right now)

D2 landed `DatabaseRouter` as a new primitive, but the gateway still uses `TenantRouter::get_or_create_any(slug)`. The smoke test confirmed this composite slug works correctly. Replacing TenantRouter with DatabaseRouter is a refactor that doesn't change behavior — defer it to a later cleanup bead. Not in this bead's scope.

## Scope summary

This bead does exactly three things:

1. Install B1's JWT middleware in the data-plane router, optional path (Authorization header present)
2. Populate F1's AuditAttribution at every audit write site when ResolvedIdentity is present
3. Migrate gRPC service.rs from x-axon-database header to the composite slug + optional JWT metadata

## Tests for this bead

### Integration test: crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs (NEW)

- create a JwtIssuer with a known key
- mint a JWT for a default user with grants on (default, default)
- start the in-process test server harness that gateway_test.rs uses
- send a request with Authorization: Bearer <jwt> to /tenants/default/databases/default/collections
- assert 200 (the JWT pipeline accepted + the handler saw ResolvedIdentity)

### Integration test: attribution populated

- send an entity-create request with a JWT
- query the audit log
- assert the audit entry has `attribution.user_id` set to the JWT sub

### gRPC test: composite slug

- send a CreateEntity gRPC request with metadata `x-axon-tenant-database: acme:orders`
- assert routing to the acme:orders database (check the storage file exists)

### Manual smoke test

Start the server, issue a JWT via `/control/tenants/default/credentials`, use the HttpAxonClient with the JWT, create an entity, verify the audit log has attribution. Write a short shell script under `scripts/cutover-smoke.sh` that does this end-to-end.

## Out of scope

- DatabaseRouter replacement — deferred
- OIDC federation — follow-up
- Signing key rotation — future ADR
- Removing legacy Identity/Role types from auth.rs — they still serve the --no-auth and Tailscale paths

- crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs — install JWT middleware, thread AuditAttribution into audit writes (10-20 sites)

- crates/axon-server/src/service.rs — rename header constant, parse composite, add JWT path
- crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs — NEW

- crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs — legacy Tailscale path, out of scope for removal
- crates/axon-storage/* — no storage changes
- crates/axon-core/* — no type changes

./cli/internal/agent/testdata/reviewer/reviewer-stream-oversized-body.jsonl:1:{"id":"as-bc4b0e90","timestamp":"2026-04-15T01:50:16.151938804Z","harness":"claude","surface":"claude","canonical_target":"claude-sonnet-4-6","model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","prompt_len":13868,"prompt":"<execute-bead>
  <bead id="axon-dccbdf73">
    <title>auth-jwt-pipeline: sign + verify with revocation cache + request extension</title>
    <description>
Implement the full JWT verification middleware in axon-server.

## What you're building

An axum middleware layer that runs on every data-plane request. It validates the JWT, installs a (user_id, tenant_id, grants) tuple into the request extension, and returns a typed AuthError on any failure. Downstream handlers read the extension and trust it completely.

## Scope — exactly what to add

### New file: `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`

A module exposing two things:

1. A `JwtIssuer` helper that signs a `JwtClaims` into a compact JWT string. HS256 is the only algorithm required for this bead. The signing key is a `Vec<u8>` passed in at construction. Provide `issue(&self, claims: &JwtClaims) -> Result<String, AuthError>` and a `verify(&self, token: &str) -> Result<JwtClaims, AuthError>` for round-tripping in tests.

2. An axum middleware `async fn jwt_verify_layer` that runs the 8-step verification order below. On success, installs a `ResolvedIdentity { user_id, tenant_id, grants }` into the request extension. On failure, returns a response built via `AuthError::into_response()` so the mapping to HTTP status + error.code is consistent.

The middleware also owns an `InMemoryRevocationCache` (a small `tokio::sync::RwLock<HashSet<Uuid>>` is fine; nothing fancy) and falls back to SQL (via the storage adapter's new `is_jti_revoked` method) on cache miss.

### Extend: `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs`

- Add `ResolvedIdentity { pub user_id: UserId, pub tenant_id: TenantId, pub grants: Grants }`.
- Add `impl AuthError` with `pub fn status_code(&self) -> u16` returning 401 or 403 per ADR-018 §4 failure table, and `pub fn error_code(&self) -> &'static str` returning the stable code string. (An axum `IntoResponse` impl can live in axon-server.)
- Add `impl Grants` with `pub fn find_database(&self, name: &str) -> Option<&GrantedDatabase>` and `impl GrantedDatabase` with `pub fn has_op(&self, op: Op) -> bool`.

### Extend: `crates/axon-storage/src/adapter.rs` (trait)

Add one method to the `StorageAdapter` trait:

```rust
async fn is_jti_revoked(&self, jti: Uuid) -> Result<bool, AxonError>;
```

Implement it in `crates/axon-storage/src/memory.rs` (store a `HashSet<Uuid>`; default-empty), `crates/axon-storage/src/sqlite.rs`, and `crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs` (`SELECT 1 FROM credential_revocations WHERE jti = $1`). The A1 schema (`auth_schema.rs`) already has the `credential_revocations` table — just query it.

### Wire into: `crates/axon-server/src/lib.rs`

Re-export `auth_pipeline::{jwt_verify_layer, JwtIssuer, InMemoryRevocationCache}`. Don't wire it into the main router yet — that's D-phase work. For this bead, the middleware just needs to exist and be testable in isolation.

## Verification order (normative — ADR-018 §4)

1. Extract `Authorization: Bearer <jwt>` from the request. Missing header → `AuthError::Unauthenticated` (401). The `--no-auth` bypass is NOT part of this bead — that's E2.
2. Parse and verify the JWT signature with HS256 against the issuer's key. Signature invalid → `CredentialInvalid` (401). Malformed structure → `CredentialMalformed` (401). `aud` as a JSON array → `CredentialMalformed` (this falls out of the A2 `parse_claims` wrapper — use it).
3. Check `nbf` and `exp` against `SystemTime::now()` with a 30s skew budget. `exp` more than 30s in the past → `CredentialExpired` (401). `nbf` more than 30s in the future → `CredentialNotYetValid` (401).
4. Check `claims.jti` against the revocation cache, then SQL on miss. Hit → `CredentialRevoked` (401). Populate the cache on SQL hit to speed up subsequent checks.
5. Compare `claims.aud` to the `{tenant}` segment extracted from the URL path. Mismatch → `CredentialWrongTenant` (**403**, not 401 — see the table). The URL extraction path is: look at `request.uri().path()` and parse the leading `/tenants/{tenant}/databases/{database}/...` segments. For this bead, implement a small helper `fn extract_tenant_database(path: &str) -> Option<(&str, &str)>` that returns `None` for non-data-plane paths so they skip the middleware.
6. Look up the `sub` UUID in the `users` table via a new `StorageAdapter::get_user(UserId) -> Result<Option<User>, AxonError>` method. User not found or `user.suspended_at_ms.is_some()` → `UserSuspended` (401).
7. Check tenant membership: a new `StorageAdapter::get_tenant_member(TenantId, UserId) -> Result<Option<TenantMember>, AxonError>`. Missing → `NotATenantMember` (403).
8. Walk `claims.grants.databases[]` looking for an entry matching the URL `{database}`. None → `DatabaseNotGranted` (403). Found → compute the required op from the HTTP method (GET = Read, POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE = Write) and intersect with the grant's ops. Empty intersection → `OpNotGranted` (403). Admin-requiring routes are out of scope for this bead — the middleware only handles Read/Write classification.
9. Build `ResolvedIdentity` and install it via `request.extensions_mut().insert(resolved)`.

## Error mapping — must exactly match ADR-018 §4

The mapping table is copied here verbatim. Do not invent variants; the 14 AuthError variants already exist in axon-core.

| Failure | Status | `error.code` |
|---|---|---|
| No Authorization header | 401 | `unauthenticated` |
| Header not `Bearer <token>` or base64 broken | 401 | `credential_malformed` |
| JWT structurally invalid / missing claims | 401 | `credential_malformed` |
| `aud` as JSON array | 401 | `credential_malformed` |
| Signature invalid | 401 | `credential_invalid` |
| `exp` in past (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_expired` |
| `nbf` in future (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_not_yet_valid` |
| `jti` revoked | 401 | `credential_revoked` |
| `iss` unknown | 401 | `credential_foreign_issuer` — for this bead, reject if `claims.iss != issuer.issuer_id` |
| `aud` ≠ URL tenant | **403** | `credential_wrong_tenant` |
| `sub` suspended/deleted | 401 | `user_suspended` |
| `sub` not a tenant member | **403** | `not_a_tenant_member` |
| URL database not in grants | **403** | `database_not_granted` |
| Op not in grant | **403** | `op_not_granted` |

Note the 401 vs 403 split: credential-level problems are 401, scope-level problems are 403.

## Testing — all must pass for this bead to be complete

### Unit tests in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_test.rs`

For every row of the error mapping table, build the minimal defective request and assert the middleware produces exactly that `(status, error_code)` pair. Table-driven with `#[test]` or a single `#[test]` that iterates an array of cases.

Additionally:

- `jwt_roundtrip_succeeds`: issue a valid JWT with `JwtIssuer::issue`, verify it with `JwtIssuer::verify`, assert claims match.
- `valid_jwt_populates_request_extension`: run the full middleware on a valid JWT, assert the response is 200 and the handler sees `ResolvedIdentity` in extensions.
- `revocation_takes_effect_within_one_second`: issue a valid JWT, verify it succeeds, insert the jti into the revocation cache, verify the next request returns 401 `credential_revoked`. Do NOT use sleep — invalidate the cache directly.
- `clock_skew_30s_accepted_31s_rejected`: construct a JWT with `exp` at `now - 29s` (should succeed) and one with `exp` at `now - 31s` (should fail with `credential_expired`). Do the same for `nbf`.
- `ops_matrix`: for each HTTP method in {GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE} and each op set in the grant, assert the expected 200/403 outcome.

### Integration test in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_integration_test.rs`

Build a minimal axum router with one dummy route `GET /tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping`. Install the middleware. Hit it with:
- No auth header → 401 `unauthenticated`
- Valid JWT with `grants: [{name: "orders", ops: [read]}]` → 200
- Valid JWT with the wrong tenant in aud → 403 `credential_wrong_tenant`
- Valid JWT with grants for a different database → 403 `database_not_granted`

### axon-sim workload `inv_018_grant_enforcement`

**If this is too large for one pass, skip the axon-sim workload and note it in the commit message for a follow-up bead.** The core verification must ship; the simulation workload can follow. Do not block the whole bead on the workload.

## Crate dependencies you will need

Add to `crates/axon-server/Cargo.toml` under `[dependencies]`:
```toml
jsonwebtoken = "9"
```

Everything else (axum, tower, tokio, uuid, serde_json, http) is already in the workspace.

## Context you must know about

### A1 landed: schema
- `crates/axon-storage/src/auth_schema.rs` has the SQL for `users`, `user_identities`, `tenant_users`, `credential_revocations`, etc.
- `user_identities.external_id` (NOT `subject`) — already corrected.
- Migration runs via `apply_auth_migrations_sqlite` / `_postgres`.

### A2 landed: types
- `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` has all the types: `TenantId`, `UserId`, `User`, `UserIdentity`, `Tenant`, `TenantMember`, `TenantRole`, `Op`, `GrantedDatabase`, `Grants`, `JwtClaims`, `AuthError` (14 variants).
- `parse_claims(&str) -> Result<JwtClaims, AuthError>` wraps serde_json and maps errors to `CredentialMalformed`. USE IT.
- `AUTH_ERROR_VARIANT_COUNT == 14` is enforced via compile-time exhaustive match.
- Delegation helper on `TenantRole`: `admin.can_delegate(op)` / etc.

### Files you should NOT touch
- Anything outside `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`, `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` (additive), `crates/axon-storage/src/{adapter,memory,sqlite,postgres}.rs` (additive), and the two new test files.
- The existing `crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs` — that's the Tailscale whois path. Leave it alone. Your pipeline is a new module.

## Quality bar

You are the implementation step. A post-merge review with opus will verify the result against the AC. If your commit misses an AC item, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher tier. **Don't commit partial work hoping the review slides.** If you cannot complete the bead in this pass, write a `no_changes_rationale.txt` explaining exactly what's done and what's blocking, and do NOT commit — the bead will be re-queued.

Specifically: if you cannot make `cargo test -p axon-server` and `cargo clippy -p axon-server -- -D warnings` green, do not commit. Fix the issues first.

- [ ] Clock skew test passes at 30s boundary (both directions)

- [ ] cargo test -p axon-core green (new helpers added)

You are a coding agent implementing a single bead inside an isolated git worktree. Work from the bead description above — it is complete and unambiguous. The AC list is the completion contract.

## How to work

1. **Read first, act second.** Read the governing ADR-018 sections and the existing crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs so you understand the AuthError variants and the parse_claims wrapper. Then read crates/axon-storage/src/auth_schema.rs to confirm the credential_revocations table shape. Then start coding.

2. **Cross-reference your work against the AC as you go.** Every AC checkbox must be provably satisfied by a piece of code you can point to. Before committing, re-read the AC and physically tick each one.

3. **Run the verification commands locally before committing.** cargo test and cargo clippy per the AC. If either fails, fix it. Do not commit red code.

4. **Commit once, when everything works.** Do not commit partial or exploratory changes. The bead is a single atomic unit of work.

5. **If you cannot complete the bead**, write `.ddx/executions/b1-enhanced-manual/no_changes_rationale.txt` with (a) what's done, (b) what's blocking, (c) why a follow-up bead is needed. Do not commit red code to "make progress".

## Constraints

- Work only in the files listed under "Scope" and "Files you should NOT touch" above.
- Do not modify CLAUDE.md or add new documentation.
- Do not run `ddx init`.
- Keep the `.ddx/executions/` directory intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- Commit with `git add <specific files> && git commit -m 'feat(auth-pipeline): ... [axon-dccbdf73]'`. Do not `git add -A` — there may be other WIP in the worktree.

## Done definition

`cargo test -p axon-server -p axon-core -p axon-storage` green + `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings` clean + every AC item ticked + one commit. That's done.

apply_auth_migrations_sqlite(conn: &rusqlite::Connection) -> Result<(), String> {\n19\t    // Create tenants table.\n20\t    // UNIQUE(name) enforced by the UNIQUE constraint on the name column.\n21\t    conn.execute_batch(\n22\t        \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tenants (\n23\t            id                 TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n24\t            name               TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,\n25\t            display_name       TEXT NOT NULL,\n26\t            created_at_ms      INTEGER NOT NULL,\n27\t            updated_at_ms      INTEGER NOT NULL\n28\t        )\",\n29\t    )\n30\t    .map_err(|e| KEY,\n36\t            display_name       TEXT NOT NULL,\n37\t            email              TEXT,\n38\t            created_at_ms      INTEGER NOT NULL,\n39\t            suspended_at_ms    INTEGER\n40\t        )\",\n41\t    )\n42\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\"users table: {e}\"))?;\n43\t\n44\t    // Create user_identities table.\n45\t    // PRIMARY KEY (provider, external_id) enforces UNIQUE(provider, external_id).\n46\t    conn.execute_batch(\n47\t        \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_identities (\n48\t            provider       TEXT NOT NULL,\n49\t            external_id    TEXT NOT NULL,\n50\t            user_id        TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),\n51\t            created_at_ms  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n52\t            PRIMARY KEY (provider, external_id)\n53\t        )\",\n54\t    )\n55\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\"user_identities table: {e}\"))?;\n56\t\n57\t    // Create tenant_users table.\n58\t    // PRIMARY KEY (tenant_id, user_id) is the composite PK required )\n68\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\"tenant_users table: {e}\"))?;\n69\t\n70\t    // Create tenant_databases table.\n71\t    conn.execute_batch(\n72\t        \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tenant_databases {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01DGpwMtL9EAnaRPBK3GqEBs","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Axon server — HTTP gateway, gRPC service, MCP stdio, and authentication.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! # Authentication\n4\t//!\n5\t//! Identity is resolved once per request by [`auth::AuthContext`] and\n6\t//! injected as a typed extension.  Three modes are supported:\n7\t//!\n8\t//! | Mode | Flag | Actor | Role |\n9\t//! |------|------|-------|------|\n10\t//! | `NoAuth` | `--no-auth` | `\"anonymous\"` | Admin |\n11\t//! | `Tailscale` | *(default)* | node name | from ACL tags |\n12\t//! | `Guest` | `--guest-role <role>` | `\"guest\"` | configured role |\n13\t//!\n14\t//! In `Tailscale` mode the server contacts the local Tailscale daemon over its\n15\t//! Unix socket (`/run/tailscale/tailscaled.sock` by default) and calls\n16\t//! `/localapi/v0/whois?addr=<peer>` to resolve the connecting node's identity.\n17\t//! Resolved identities are cached by peer IP for the duration of\n18\t//! `--auth-cache-ttl-secs` (default 60 s).\n19\t//!\n20\t//! ACL tag → role mapping:\n21\t//! - `tag:axon-admin` → [`Role::Admin`]\n22\t//! - `tag:axon-write` / `tag:axon-agent` → [`Role::Write`]\n23\t//! - `tag:axon-read` → [`Role::Read`]\n24\t//! - no matching tag → `--tailscale-default-role` (default `read`)\n25\t//!\n26\t//! Connections that are not on the tailnet are rejected with HTTP 401 / gRPC\n27\t//! `UNAUTHENTICATED`.  If the Tailscale daemon is unreachable the server\n28\t//! returns HTTP 503 / gRPC `UNAVAILABLE`.\n29\t//!\n30\t//! # Request flow\n31\t//!\n32\t//! ```text\n33\t//! TCP accept\n34\t//!   └─ authenticate_http_request  (gateway.rs middleware)\n35\t//!        ├─ AuthContext::resolve_peer  →  Identity\n36\t//!        └─ insert Identity into request extensions\n37\t//!             └─ route handler\n38\t//!                  ├─ extract Extension<Identity>\n39\t//!                  ├─ identity.require_read() / require_write() / require_admin()\n40\t//!                  └─ handler logic\n41\t//! ```\n42\t//!\n43\t//! gRPC follows the same pattern via [`service::AxonServiceImpl::authorize`].\n44\tpub mod actor_scope;\n45\tpub mod auth;\n46\tmod collection_listing;\n47\tpub(crate) mod embedded_ui;\n48\tpub mod control_plane;\n49\tpub mod control_plane_routes;\n50\tpub mod cors_config;\n51\tpub mod user_roles;\n52\tpub mod gateway;\n53\tpub mod idempotency;\n54\tpub(crate) mod mcp_http;\n55\tpub mod mcp_stdio;\n56\tpub mod rate_limit;\n57\tpub mod schema_registry;\n58\tpub mod serve;\n59\tpub mod service;\n60\tpub mod tenant_router;\n61\t\n62\tpub use auth::{AuthContext, AuthMode, Identity, Role};\n63\tpub use mcp_stdio::run_mcp_stdio;\n64\tpub use service::AxonServiceImpl;\n65\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01TSMSYvQzdh78YH3Sxyiy8q","session_id":"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2","uuid":"b2e10ba8-f883-42a2-b408-1004661a2ce3","timestamp":"2026-04-15T01:30:53.127Z"}

     let key = self.resolve_catalog_key(&entity.collection)?;\n        // Read current version.\n        let current = self.get(&entity.collection, &entity.id)?;\n        let actual_version = current.as_ref().map(|e| e.version).unwrap_or(0);\n\n        if actual_version != expected_version {\n            return Err(AxonError::ConflictingVersion {\n                expected: expected_version,\n                actual: actual_version,\n                current_entity: current.map(Box::new),\n            });\n        }\n\n        let new_version = expected_version + 1;\n        let data_json = serde_json::to_value(&entity.data)?;\n\n        let changed = self.block(self.client.execute(\n            \"UPDATE entities SET version = $3, data = $4\n                 WHERE collection = $1 AND database_name = $5 AND schema_name = $6 AND id = $2 AND version = $7\",\n            &[\n                &key.collection.as_str(),\n                &entity.id.as_str(),\n                &(new_version as i64),\n                &data_json,\n                &key.namespace.database,\n                &key.namespace.schema,\n                &(expected_version as i64),\n            ],\n        ))?;\n\n        if changed == 0 {\n            let current_after_race = self.get(&entity.collection, &entity.id)?;\n            let actual = current_after_race.as_ref().map(|e| e.version).unwrap_or(0);\n            return Err(AxonError::ConflictingVersion {\n                expected: expected_version,\n                actual,\n                current_entity: current_after_race.map(Box::new),\n            });\n        }\n\n        Ok(Entity {\n            collection: key.collection,\n            version: new_version,\n            ..entity\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn create_if_absent(\n        &mut self,\n        entity: Entity,\n        expected_absent_version: u64,\n    ) -> Result<Entity, AxonError> {\n        let key = (collection, database_name, schema_name, id, version, data)\n                 VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5, $6)\n                 ON CONFLICT (database_name, schema_name, collection, id) DO NOTHING\",\n            &[\n                &key.collection.as_str(),\n                &key.namespace.database,\n                &key.namespace.schema,\n                &entity.id.as_str(),\n                &(entity.version as i64),\n                &data_json,\n            ],\n        ))?;\n\n        if changed == 0 {\n            let current = self.get(&entity.collection, &entity.id)?;\n            let actual = current\n                .as_ref()\n                .map(|existing| existing.version)\n                .unwrap_or(0);\n            return Err(AxonError::ConflictingVersion {\n &CollectionId,\n        namespace: &Namespace,\n    ) -> Result<bool, AxonError> {\n        self.collection_exists_in_namespace(collection, namespace)\n    }\n}\n\n// ── Per-tenant PostgreSQL provisioning ───────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Validate a PostgreSQL database name to prevent SQL injection in DDL.\n///\n/// PostgreSQL identifiers may contain letters, digits, `_`, and `$`, and\n/// must start with a letter or `_`. We apply a conservative subset of that\n/// rule here to keep things simple and safe.\nfn validate_pg_db_name(name: &str) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n    if name.is_empty() {\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(\n            \"database name must not be empty\".to_owned(),\n        ));\n    }\n    let first = name.chars().next().expect(\"non-empty checked above\");\n    if !first.is_ascii_alphabetic() && first != '_' {\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\n            \"database name '{name}' must start with a letter or underscore\"\n        )));\n    }\n    if !name.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || c == '_') {\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\n            \"database name '{name}' contains invalid characters (only ASCII alphanumeric and _ allowed)\"\n        )));\n    }\n    Ok(())\n}\n\n/// Derive a per-tenant DSN from a superadmin DSN by replacing/adding the\n/// `dbname` key.\n///\n/// The superadmin DSN uses the `postgres` maintenance database.  We produce a\n/// new DSN that targets `axon_{name}`.\n///\n/// Both libpq keyword–value format (`host=... dbname=...`) and URL format\n/// (`postgres://...`) are supported.\npub fn tenant_dsn(superadmin_dsn: &str, tenant_db_name: &str) -> String {\n    let target = format!(\"axon_{tenant_db_name}\");\n    if superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\"postgres://\") || superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\"postgresql://\") {\n        // URL format: replace path component.\n        // e.g. postgres://user:pass@host/somedb?sslmode=disable\n        if let Some(pos) = superadmin_dsn.find(\"://\") {\n            let after_scheme = &superadmin_dsn[pos + 3..];\n            // Find the slash that separates authority from path.\n            if let Some(slash_pos) = after_scheme.find('/') {\n                let scheme_authority = &superadmin_dsn[..pos + 3 + slash_pos];\n                let rest = &after_scheme[slash_pos + 1..];\n                // Strip existing dbname (everything before '?').\n                let query = if let Some(q) = rest.find('?') {\n                    &rest[q..]\n                } else {\n                    \"\"\n                };\n                return format!(\"{scheme_authority}/{target}{query}\");\n            }\n            // No path: append the target database.\n            return format!(\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\");\n        }\n        format!(\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\")\n    } else {\n        // Keyword–value format.  Replace existing dbname= or append it.\n        let mut parts: Vec<String> = superadmin_dsn\n            .split_whitespace()\n            .filter(|part| !part.starts_with(\"dbname=\"))\n            .map(str::to_owned)\n            .collect();\n        parts.push(format!(\"dbname={target}\"));\n        parts.join(\" \")\n    }\n}\n\n/// Create a physical PostgreSQL database named `axon_{name}` using a\n/// superadmin connection.\n///\n/// The `superadmin_dsn` must connect to the `postgres` maintenance database\n/// (or any database the superuser can reach) with sufficient privileges to\n/// execute `CREATE DATABASE`.\n///\n/// The database name is validated to prevent SQL injection.\n///\n/// # Errors\n///\n/// Returns `AxonError::AlreadyExists` if the database already exists.\n/// Returns `AxonError::InvalidArgument` if `name` contains invalid characters.\n/// Returns `AxonError::Storage` for other PostgreSQL errors.\npub fn provision_postgres_database(superadmin_dsn: &str, name: &str) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n    validate_pg_db_name(name)?;\n    let full_name = format!(\"axon_{name}\");\n    let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\n        .enable_all()\n        .build()\n        .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n    rt.block_on(async {\n        let (client, connection) = tokio_postgres::connect(superadmin_dsn, NoTls)\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\n            let _ = connection.await;\n        });\n        let row = client\n            .query_one(\n                \"SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM pg_database WHERE datname = $1)\",\n                &[&full_name.as_str()],\n            )\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n        let exists: bool = row.get(0);\n        if exists {\n            return Err(AxonError::AlreadyExists(format!(\n                \"PostgreSQL database '{full_name}'\"\n            )));\n        }\n        // CREATE DATABASE cannot run inside a transaction; use simple_query to\n        // avoid the implicit transaction that execute() would open.\n        client\n            .simple_query(&format!(\"CREATE DATABASE \\\"{full_name}\\\"\"))\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n        Ok(())\n    })\n}\n\n/// Drop the physical PostgreSQL database named `axon_{name}`.\n///\n/// The `superadmin_dsn` must have sufficient privileges to execute\n/// `DROP DATABASE`.\n///\n/// # Errors\n///\n/// Returns `AxonError::NotFound` if the database does not exist.\n/// Returns `AxonError::InvalidArgument` if `name` contains invalid characters.\n/// Returns `AxonError::Storage` for other PostgreSQL errors.\npub fn deprovision_postgres_database(superadmin_dsn: &str, name: &str) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n    validate_pg_db_name(name)?;\n    let full_name = format!(\"axon_{name}\");\n    let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\n        .enable_all()\n        .build()\n        .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n    rt.block_on(async {\n        let (client, connection) = tokio_postgres::connect(superadmin_dsn, NoTls)\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\n            let _ = connection.await;\n        });\n        let row = client\n            .query_one(\n                \"SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM pg_database WHERE datname = $1)\",\n                &[&full_name.as_str()],\n            )\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n        let exists: bool = row.get(0);\n        if !exists {\n            return Err(AxonError::NotFound(format!(\n                \"PostgreSQL database '{full_name}'\"\n            )));\n        }\n        client\n            .simple_query(&format!(\"DROP DATABASE \\\"{full_name}\\\"\"))\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n        Ok(())\n    })\n}\n\n// The conformance test suite requires PostgreSQL.\n// Explicit verification path:\n// AXON_TEST_POSTGRES=\"host=localhost user=axon dbname=axon_test\" cargo test -p axon-storage postgres::tests:: -- --nocapture\n// When `AXON_TEST_POSTGRES` is unset, these tests attempt to provision PostgreSQL\n// via testcontainers and skip cleanly if the container runtime is unavailable.\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use std::{\n        ops::{Deref, DerefMut},\n        sync::{Mutex, MutexGuard, OnceLock},\n    };\n\n    use super::*;\n    use axon_core::types::Link;\n    use testcontainers_modules::postgres::Postgres as PgContainer;\n    use testcontainers_modules::testcontainers::{runners::SyncRunner, Container};\n\n    struct TestDatabase {\n        url: String,\n        _container: Option<Container<PgContainer>>,\n    }\n\n    enum TestSetupError {\n        Skip(String),\n        Fail(AxonError),\n    }\n\n    impl TestDatabase {\n        fn connect() -> Result<Self, TestSetupError> {\n            if let Ok(url) = std::env::var(\"AXON_TEST_POSTGRES\") {\n                return Ok(Self {\n                    url,\n                    _container: None,\n                });\n            }\n\n            let container = PgContainer::default()\n                .with_db_name(\"axon_test\")\n                .with_user(\"postgres\")\n                .with_password(\"postgres\")\n                .start()\n                .map_err(|error| {\n                    TestSetupError::Skip(format!(\n                        \"AXON_TEST_POSTGRES is unset and PostgreSQL test container startup failed: {error}\"\n                    ))\n                })?;\n            let host = container.get_host().map_err(|error| {\n                TestSetupError::Fail(AxonError::Storage(format!(\n                    \"failed to resolve PostgreSQL test container host: {error}\"\n                )))\n            })?;\n            let port = container.get_host_port_ipv4(5432).map_err(|error| {\n                TestSetupError::Fail(AxonError::Storage(format!(\n                    \"failed to resolve PostgreSQL test container port: {error}\"\n                )))\n            })?;\n\n            Ok(Self {\n                url: format!(\n                    \"host={host} port={port} user=postgres password=postgres dbname=axon_test\"\n                ),\n                _container: Some(container),\n            })\n        }\n\n        fn url(&self) -> &str {\n            &self.url\n        }\n    }\n\n    struct TestStore {\n        adapter: PostgresStorageAdapter,\n        _database: TestDatabase,\n    }\n\n    impl Deref for TestStore {\n        type Target = PostgresStorageAdapter;\n\n        fn deref(&self) -> &Self::Target {\n            &self.adapter\n        }\n    }\n\n    impl DerefMut for TestStore {\n        fn deref_mut(&mut self) -> &mut Self::Target {\n            &mut self.adapter\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn postgres_test_guard() -> MutexGuard<'static, ()> {\n        static GUARD: OnceLock<Mutex<()>> = OnceLock::new();\n        GUARD\n            .get_or_init(|| Mutex::new(()))\n            .lock()\n            .expect(\"PostgreSQL test guard lock should not be poisoned\")\n    }\n\n    fn skip_postgres_test(test_name: &str, reason: &str) {\n        tracing::warn!(test = test_name, reason, \"skipping PostgreSQL storage test\");\n    }\n\n    fn database_or_skip(test_name: &str) -> Option<TestDatabase> {\n        match TestDatabase::connect() {\n            Ok(database) => Some(database),\n            Err(TestSetupError::Skip(reason)) => {\n                skip_postgres_test(test_name, &reason);\n                None\n            }\n            Err(TestSetupError::Fail(error)) => {\n                panic!(\"PostgreSQL test setup should succeed: {error}\");\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn store() -> Result<TestStore, TestSetupError> {\n        let database = TestDatabase::connect()?;\n        let adapter =\n            PostgresStorageAdapter::connect(database.url()).map_err(TestSetupError::Fail)?;\n        // Clean tables for a fresh test.\n        adapter\n            .block(adapter.client.batch_execute(\n                \"TRUNCATE entities, schemas, collection_views, collections, namespaces, databases, audit_log RESTART IDENTITY CASCADE\",\n            ))\n            .map_err(TestSetupError::Fail)?;\n        adapter\n            .ensure_default_namespace()\n            .map_err(TestSetupError::Fail)?;\n        Ok(TestStore {\n            adapter,\n            _database: database,\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn store_or_skip(test_name: &str) -> Option<TestStore> {\n        match store() {\n            Ok(store) => Some(store),\n            Err(TestSetupError::Skip(reason)) => {\n                skip_postgres_test(test_name, &reason);\n                None\n            }\n            Err(TestSetupError::Fail(error)) => {\n                panic!(\"PostgreSQL test setup should succeed: {error}\");\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn register_unique_namespaced_collection(\n        store: &mut TestStore,\n        qualified: &CollectionId,\n    ) -> (Namespace, CollectionId) {\n        let (namespace, bare_collection) = Namespace::parse(qualified.as_str());\n        let bare_collection = CollectionId::new(bare_collection);\n\n        store\n            .create_database(namespace.database.as_str())\n            .expect(\"database create should succeed\");\n        store\n            .create_namespace(&namespace)\n            .expect(\"namespace create should succeed\");\n        store\n            .register_collection_in_namespace(&bare_collection, &namespace)\n            .expect(\"collection register should succeed\");\n\n tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\n            .enable_all()\n            .build()\n            .expect(\"test runtime\");\n        let (client, connection) = rt\n            .block_on(tokio_postgres::connect(url, NoTls))\n            .expect(\"test connection\");\n        rt.spawn(async move {\n            let _ = connection.await;\n        });\n        (client, rt)\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn postgres_roundtrip_when_available() {\n        let _guard = postgres_test_guard();\n        let Some(mut s) = store_or_skip(\"postgres_roundtrip_when_available\") else {\n serde_json::json!({\"title\": \"hello\"}),\n        );\n        s.put(entity).expect(\"test operation should succeed\");\n        let got = s\n            .get(&col, &EntityId::new(\"t-001\"))\n            .expect(\"test operation should succeed\")\n            .expect(\"test operation should succeed\");\n        assert_eq!(got.data[\"title\"], \"hello\");\n        assert_eq!(got.version, 1);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn unregister_collection_cleans_up_legacy_collection_views_when_available() {\n        let _guard = postgres_test_guard();\n        let Some(database) = database_or_skip(\n
